frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
generated_comment: Yaar, I told you from day one — when a girl comes from that kind of family, with those kinds of expectations, you have to understand what the real plan is. These days marriage has become a business for them — spend a few years, collect evidence of whatever they can, then cash out. Alimony, house, car — yeh sab toh reverse dowry hai, bhai. Our fathers gave dowry to take a wife, now we give dowry to get rid of one. We should have made everyone sign a prenup, but no, back then we all thought 'our wives are different.' They are all the same when the lawyer enters the picture.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment generalizes all women as financially calculating and strategically deceptive in marriage, framing alimony as a deliberate scheme rather than a legal protection. It encodes gender-based essentialism ('they are all the same') and positions women's financial agency in divorce as inherently predatory, which is a covert misogynistic framing because it presents itself as worldly wisdom rather than explicit hostility.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married for 18 years. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are sitting with three male friends at your farmhouse on a Saturday evening having drinks after one of your friends just got served divorce papers by his wife. Your friend is telling the group what his wife is demanding in the settlement. You are responding and sharing your views on what women want from marriage and what this particular woman's real intentions were from the beginning.
